toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try to fetch debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server for this BuildId
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try ELF buildid convention on Microsoft symbol server
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" 2>&1
echo ""
# Also try the debug file variant
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}.dbg" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try NuGet symbol server too
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" 2>&1
arguments.description: Try fetching symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Try fetching symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this Android tombstone. The key information is:

1. The library is `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`
2. There are several frames with PC offsets that need to be symbolicated

To symbolicate these, I would need:
- The debug symbols for this specific build of `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (matching the BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`)


- Tools like `addr2line` or `llvm-symbolizer` to resolve the addresses

The challenge is that I don't have access to the actual debug symbols for this specific build, the .NET runtime symbol server, or the original binary with debug information. Without the symbols matching that BuildId, I can't properly symbolicate these frames, though I could potentially check public symbol servers for the information.

Microsoft does publish .NET runtime symbols on their symbol server, and I could try looking up this BuildId there using the standard ELF symbol server path format or the `dotnet-symbol` tool. Let me see if those tools are available first, though I'm realizing this might be a test file where the BuildId may not actually exist on any public server. I'll try downloading the symbols from Microsoft's server even though this appears to be test data with a BuildId that probably doesn't correspond to a real published build.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer dotnet-symbol 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i binutils 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolArgs.command:
# Try to fetch debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server for this BuildId
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try ELF buildid convention on Microsoft symbol server
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" 2>&1
echo ""
# Also try the debug file variant
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}.dbg" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try NuGet symbol server too
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" 2>&1

data.content: The symbol server has this binary. Let me download it and symbolicate the frames.

arguments.command:
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"
WORKDIR="/tmp/sv-c87bc015efcb405c8aa7df0b1847fdb5"

# Download the library
curl -sfSL -o "${WORKDIR}/${FILENAME}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"
file "${WORKDIR}/${FILENAME}"
# Check if it has debug info
readelf -S "${WORKDIR}/${FILENAME}" | grep -i debug | head -5
